interpretation: Verifier-first arm ran end-to-end on real kernel functions. CBMC (the oracle) stayed sound throughout — it never falsely claimed safe. 0 real bug candidates. Dominant bottleneck is LLM harness-synthesis quality on a 7B CPU model: 26/54 functions could not be extracted by the heuristic source slicer; of the harnesses that were generated, the LLM commonly (a) omitted the function-under-test body (all 4 "unsafe" verdicts were CBMC "no body for callee" — reclassified to harness-invalid), or (b) stubbed callees so CBMC had no memory operation to check (-> inconclusive). The 22 inconclusive are buildable harnesses where the function reduces to calls into stubbed externs. No function was proven UNSAFE through a well-formed harness, so no bug candidate was produced.
soundness_note: This is the designed behavior: the LLM is structurally a proposer (harness + contracts), CBMC is the verdict authority. A weak proposer yields no findings but never an unsound "safe". To raise yield: better source extraction (clang/libclang instead of regex), a stronger harness-author model, and inlining real callee bodies instead of extern stubs.
